sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The Dropbox connector syncs files and folder metadata from a Dropbox account into the Shielva knowledge base using the Dropbox API v2. It is used by teams who store documents, assets, and files in Dropbox and want to make that content searchable and queryable via Shielva's AI layer.

**Key capabilities:**
- Full recursive listing of all files and folders from the Dropbox root using cursor-based pagination (`list_folder` + `list_folder_continue`)
- File metadata retrieval by path
- Full-text search over Dropbox files via `files/search_v2`
- List and inspect shared links
- Account space usage reporting
- Team member listing (Dropbox Business / team apps)
- OAuth2 authorization URL generation with `token_access_type=offline` for refresh tokens
- Circuit breaker (`CircuitBreaker`, threshold: 5 failures) on health checks to prevent cascading failures
- Automatic retry via `with_retry()` on transient errors
- Supports both `client_id`/`client_secret` field names and legacy `app_key`/`app_secret` for backward compatibility

**Auth model:** OAuth2 — authorization code flow. Connector generates an authorization URL; after user consent, an access token (and optionally refresh token) is stored in config.

**Resources synced:** Dropbox file and folder metadata entries

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Create a Dropbox App

1. Go to the [Dropbox App Console](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps).
2. Click **Create app** → choose **Scoped access** → **Full Dropbox** (or **App folder** for sandboxed access).
3. Under **Permissions**, enable: `files.metadata.read`, `sharing.read`, `account_info.read`.
4. Note the **App key** and **App secret**.
5. Under **OAuth2 Redirect URIs**, add your callback URL (e.g. `https://yourapp.com/oauth/callback`).

### 2. Install the connector (app credentials only)

```python
import asyncio
from connector import DropboxConnector

async def main():
    connector = DropboxConnector(
        tenant_id="your-tenant-id",
        connector_id="dropbox-prod",
        config={
            "client_id": "your_app_key",
            "client_secret": "your_app_secret",
            "redirect_uri": "https://yourapp.com/oauth/callback",
        },
    )
    result = await connector.install()
    print(result.message)
    # "Dropbox app credentials validated. Call authorize() to obtain an access token."

asyncio.run(main())
```

### 3. Obtain authorization URL and exchange code for token

```python
# Get the URL to redirect the user to
auth_url = connector.authorize()
print(auth_url)  # Redirect user to this URL

# After user approves, exchange the returned `code` for an access token
# via POST https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token
# Store the returned access_token (and refresh_token) in your config
```

### 4. Connect with access token and sync

```python
connector_with_token = DropboxConnector(
    tenant_id="your-tenant-id",
    connector_id="dropbox-prod",
    config={
        "client_id": "your_app_key",
        "client_secret": "your_app_secret",
        "access_token": "sl.xxxx...",
    },
)
await connector_with_token.install()  # Validates token via /users/get_current_account
result = await connector_with_token.sync(kb_id="my-kb-id")
print(f"Synced {result.documents_synced} files")
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
**Auth type:** `oauth2` — Authorization Code flow

### OAuth2 endpoints

| Step | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| Authorization URL | `https://www.dropbox.com/oauth2/authorize` |
| Token exchange | `https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token` |
| API calls | `Authorization: Bearer {access_token}` |

### Authorization flow

1. Call `connector.authorize()` to get the authorization URL.
2. Redirect the user's browser to that URL. Dropbox asks the user to approve access.
3. After approval, Dropbox redirects to your `redirect_uri` with a `code` query parameter.
4. Exchange the code for an access token via `POST https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token` with `grant_type=authorization_code`.
5. Store `access_token` (and `refresh_token` for long-lived access) in the connector config.
6. The connector uses `token_access_type=offline` in the authorization URL to request a refresh token.

### Required OAuth scopes

| Scope | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `files.metadata.read` | List folders, get file metadata |
| `sharing.read` | List shared links |
| `account_info.read` | Get current account info for health checks |

### Credential fields

| Field | Alias | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | `app_key` | Dropbox App Key from the App Console |
| `client_secret` | `app_secret` | Dropbox App Secret from the App Console |
| `access_token` | — | OAuth2 access token obtained after authorization |
| `refresh_token` | — | OAuth2 refresh token for long-lived access |
| `redirect_uri` | — | OAuth2 callback URL (must match App Console registration) |

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
Every `install_field` listed below is collected from the user **once** at install time and stored in the connector's encrypted config. `config_keys` are values the connector derives at runtime (e.g. tokens) and persists for subsequent calls.

### Install fields

| Key | Label | Type | Required | Description |
|-----|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `client_id` | App Key | `string` | ✅ | Dropbox App Key from the Dropbox App Console (https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps). |
| `client_secret` | App Secret | `password` | ✅ | Dropbox App Secret from the Dropbox App Console. |
| `redirect_uri` | Redirect URI (optional) | `string` | — | OAuth2 redirect URI. Must match a URI registered in the Dropbox App Console. |

### Runtime config keys

_No runtime-derived config keys for this connector._

### Defaults & validation

- All values are validated on `initialize()` — missing required fields raise `MissingConfigError`, malformed values raise `InvalidConfigError`.
- Numeric and boolean fields are coerced from strings (e.g. `"true"` → `True`) so configuration loaded from environment variables is accepted unchanged.
- Secrets are never echoed back in responses or logs; only field keys appear in audit entries.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: The `DropboxConnector` class exposes the following public methods.

children.title: install()
children.content:
Validates that `client_id` (`app_key`) and `client_secret` (`app_secret`) are present. If `access_token` is also present, validates it live by calling `POST /users/get_current_account`.

**Returns:** `InstallResult`

| Outcome | `health` | `auth_status` | `message` |
|---|---|---|---|
| App key or secret missing | `OFFLINE` | `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` | `"app_key and app_secret are required"` |
| Token present but rejected | `OFFLINE` | `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` | Dropbox error message |
| App creds only, no token yet | `HEALTHY` | `CONNECTED` | `"Call authorize() to obtain an access token."` |
| App creds + valid token | `HEALTHY` | `CONNECTED` | `"Connected to Dropbox"` |

```python
from connector import DropboxConnector

connector = DropboxConnector(
    tenant_id="t1",
    connector_id="dropbox-prod",
    config={"client_id": "appkey", "client_secret": "appsecret", "access_token": "sl.xxx"},
)
result = await connector.install()
print(result.message)  # "Connected to Dropbox"
```

children.title: authorize()
children.content:
Returns the Dropbox OAuth2 authorization URL. This is a synchronous method — no network call is made. The returned URL includes `token_access_type=offline` to request a refresh token.

**Returns:** `str` — the full authorization URL

Redirect the user to this URL. After approval, Dropbox redirects to `redirect_uri` with `?code=...`.

```python
auth_url = connector.authorize()
print(auth_url)
# https://www.dropbox.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=appkey&response_type=code&token_access_type=offline&redirect_uri=...
```

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
Concurrently calls `POST /users/get_current_account` and `POST /users/get_space_usage` using `asyncio.gather()`. Returns the authenticated display name and email when healthy. Updates the circuit breaker state on each call.

**Returns:** `HealthCheckResult`

| Outcome | `health` | `auth_status` | Extra fields |
|---|---|---|---|
| No `access_token` | `OFFLINE` | `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` | — |
| Token rejected (401/403) | `OFFLINE` | `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` | — |
| Network error (circuit open) | `OFFLINE` | `FAILED` | — |
| Network error (circuit closed) | `DEGRADED` | `FAILED` | — |
| Success | `HEALTHY` | `CONNECTED` | `display_name`, `email` |

```python
result = await connector.health_check()
print(result.health)        # ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY
print(result.display_name)  # "Jane Smith"
print(result.email)         # "jane@example.com"
```

children.title: sync()
children.content:
Recursively lists all files and folders from the Dropbox root using `list_folder` with `recursive=True`, following cursor pagination via `list_folder_continue`. Each entry is normalized to a `ConnectorDocument` and optionally ingested.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `full` | `bool` | `False` | Reserved for full/incremental distinction (Dropbox has no server-side `since` filter — always full) |
| `since` | `datetime \| None` | `None` | Ignored — Dropbox `list_folder` does not support a `since` parameter |
| `kb_id` | `str` | `""` | Knowledge base ID for ingestion |

**Returns:** `SyncResult`

```python
result = await connector.sync(kb_id="kb-abc")
print(result.status)              # SyncStatus.COMPLETED
print(result.documents_found)     # e.g. 1843 (total files + folders)
print(result.documents_synced)    # e.g. 1843
print(result.documents_failed)    # e.g. 0
```

**Note:** If `_fetch_all_entries()` raises a `DropboxError`, `sync()` returns `SyncStatus.FAILED` immediately. Individual normalization failures increment `documents_failed` but the sync continues.

children.title: list_folder()
children.content:
Lists the contents of a Dropbox folder. Wraps `POST /files/list_folder`.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `path` | `str` | `""` | Dropbox path (e.g. `/Documents`). Empty string means root. |
| `recursive` | `bool` | `False` | When `True`, lists all descendant files and folders |

**Returns:** `dict[str, Any]` — Dropbox list_folder response with `entries`, `cursor`, and `has_more`

```python
result = await connector.list_folder(path="/Documents", recursive=False)
for entry in result["entries"]:
    print(entry[".tag"], entry["name"])
```

children.title: list_folder_continue()
children.content:
Continues a folder listing using a cursor returned by `list_folder()`. Wraps `POST /files/list_folder/continue`.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cursor` | `str` | Cursor from a previous `list_folder()` or `list_folder_continue()` response |

**Returns:** `dict[str, Any]` — next page of entries

```python
page = await connector.list_folder(path="")
while page["has_more"]:
    page = await connector.list_folder_continue(cursor=page["cursor"])
    for entry in page["entries"]:
        print(entry["name"])
```

children.title: get_metadata()
children.content:
Retrieves metadata for a specific file or folder by its Dropbox path. Wraps `POST /files/get_metadata`.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `path` | `str` | Dropbox path, e.g. `/Reports/Q4.pdf` |

**Returns:** `dict[str, Any]` — Dropbox file or folder metadata object

```python
meta = await connector.get_metadata(path="/Reports/Q4.pdf")
print(meta["name"], meta["size"], meta["client_modified"])
```

children.title: search_files()
children.content:
Full-text search over Dropbox files via `POST /files/search_v2`.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `query` | `str` | required | Search query string |
| `max_results` | `int` | `100` | Maximum number of results to return |

**Returns:** `dict[str, Any]` — Dropbox search_v2 response with `matches` array

```python
results = await connector.search_files(query="Q4 report", max_results=20)
for match in results["matches"]:
    print(match["metadata"]["metadata"]["name"])
```

children.title: list_shared_links()
children.content:
Lists shared links for a given path, or all shared links in the account. Wraps `POST /sharing/list_shared_links`.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `path` | `str \| None` | `None` | Dropbox path to filter by. Pass `None` to list all shared links. |

**Returns:** `dict[str, Any]` — Dropbox shared links response

```python
links = await connector.list_shared_links(path="/Public")
for link in links["links"]:
    print(link["url"], link["name"])
```

children.title: get_space_usage()
children.content:
Returns storage allocation and usage for the current Dropbox account. Wraps `POST /users/get_space_usage`.

**Returns:** `dict[str, Any]` — Dropbox space usage response with `used` and `allocation` fields

```python
usage = await connector.get_space_usage()
print(f"Used: {usage['used']} bytes")
print(f"Allocated: {usage['allocation']['allocated']} bytes")
```

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content:
Every record this connector emits into the Shielva knowledge base is a `ConnectorDocument` — a normalized envelope with a stable `source_id`, a resource type discriminator, and a payload of provider-specific fields. The shape per resource is documented below; the contract is enforced by the `normalize_*` helpers in `helpers/utils.py` (or `helpers/normalizer.py`).

### Envelope fields (shared)

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `source_id` | `str` | SHA-256-derived 16-char ID, stable across resyncs |
| `resource_type` | `str` | Discriminator: `message`, `contact`, `event`, etc. |
| `payload` | `dict` | Provider-native fields, untouched JSON-safe values |
| `created_at` | `str` | RFC 3339 timestamp from the provider |
| `updated_at` | `str` | RFC 3339 timestamp from the provider |
| `tenant_id` | `str` | Multi-tenant scope — never `None` for stored records |

### Resource models

#### `InstallResult`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `health` | `ConnectorHealth` |
| `auth_status` | `AuthStatus` |
| `connector_id` | `str` |
| `message` | `str` |

#### `HealthCheckResult`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `health` | `ConnectorHealth` |
| `auth_status` | `AuthStatus` |
| `message` | `str` |
| `display_name` | `str` |
| `email` | `str` |

#### `SyncResult`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `status` | `SyncStatus` |
| `documents_found` | `int` |
| `documents_synced` | `int` |
| `documents_failed` | `int` |
| `message` | `str` |

#### `ConnectorDocument`

Normalized document emitted by the connector into the knowledge base.

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `source_id` | `str` |
| `title` | `str` |
| `content` | `str` |
| `connector_id` | `str` |
| `tenant_id` | `str` |
| `source_url` | `str` |
| `metadata` | `dict[str, Any]` |


### `source_id` determinism

All resource IDs are computed by SHA-256-hashing the provider's stable identifier (e.g. message ID, contact ID, event UUID) plus the resource type, then truncating to 16 characters. This guarantees that re-syncing the same resource produces the same Shielva document ID — enabling idempotent ingestion and incremental updates without duplicates.

sections.title: Sync Behavior
sections.content:
`sync()` is the bulk ingestion entry point. A single call pulls every resource this connector knows how to sync from Dropbox, normalizes each one into a `ConnectorDocument`, and returns a `SyncResult` summarizing what was processed.

### Sync lifecycle

1. **Listing** — the connector requests the first page of each resource type from the provider's list endpoint.
2. **Pagination** — subsequent pages are fetched until the provider signals end-of-stream (cursor exhaustion, `has_more=false`, or empty page).
3. **Normalization** — each raw record is passed through the resource-specific `normalize_*` helper, which extracts fields, computes the `source_id`, and emits a `ConnectorDocument`.
4. **Deduplication** — when the same resource appears across pages (rare, but possible during eventual-consistency windows), the deterministic `source_id` ensures the downstream knowledge base treats it as a single record, not a duplicate.
5. **Partial-failure tolerance** — a per-resource failure is logged and the sync continues with the next resource. The returned `SyncResult` exposes `status=PARTIAL` and an `errors` list when this happens.

### Pagination strategy

- **Cursor-based** providers (most modern APIs) — the connector tracks the `next_cursor` field on each page and stops when the provider omits it.
- **Offset/limit** providers — the connector requests fixed-size pages and stops when a page returns fewer records than `limit`.
- **Token-based (e.g. `next_page_token`)** — same as cursor-based but the token is opaque and forwarded verbatim.

### Rate-limit handling

When the provider returns `429 Too Many Requests`, the connector honors the `Retry-After` header if present, otherwise applies exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s capped at 30s) with full jitter. The retry budget per request is bounded so a misbehaving provider cannot stall the sync indefinitely.

### Incremental sync

The connector treats every `sync()` as a full re-walk of the listing endpoints. Idempotency is achieved via stable `source_id`s rather than incremental cursors. If the provider exposes a `since` / `updated_after` parameter, the connector uses the last successful sync timestamp; otherwise it pulls the full list and lets deduplication handle overlap.

### Features enabled

This connector is configured with: circuit_breaker, cursor_pagination, error_handling, normalizer, oauth2, rate_limiting, recursive_listing, retry, shared_links, space_usage, team_members.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
The connector defines a dedicated exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`. All exceptions carry `.message`, `.status_code`, and `.code`.

| Exception | HTTP Status | `.code` | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `DropboxError` | varies | varies | Base class for all Dropbox connector errors |
| `DropboxAuthError` | 401 / 403 | — | Access token rejected or expired |
| `DropboxRateLimitError` | 429 | `rate_limit` | Too many requests; `.retry_after` (float, seconds) |
| `DropboxNotFoundError` | 409 | `not_found` | The requested path does not exist (Dropbox uses 409 for path errors, not 404) |
| `DropboxNetworkError` | 5xx / timeout | — | Transient network failure |

**Note:** Dropbox uses HTTP 409 (not 404) for path-not-found errors. `DropboxNotFoundError` has `status_code=409`.

### How the connector handles each error

- **`DropboxAuthError`** — caught in `install()` and `health_check()`, returned as `AuthStatus.INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. Circuit breaker increments.
- **`DropboxNetworkError`** — caught in `health_check()`. If the circuit breaker is open (>= 5 consecutive failures), returns `ConnectorHealth.OFFLINE`; otherwise `DEGRADED`.
- **`DropboxError` in `sync()`** — if `_fetch_all_entries()` raises, `sync()` returns `SyncStatus.FAILED`. Individual entry normalization errors increment `documents_failed` but do not stop the sync.
- **`DropboxRateLimitError`** — raised on 429. The `with_retry()` helper honours `retry_after` before re-attempting.

sections.title: Testing
sections.content:
Every connector ships with a unit test suite under `tests/`. Unit tests mock the HTTP layer so they run offline; integration tests (if configured) hit the live provider API and require valid credentials in the environment.

### Running unit tests

```bash
cd dropbox_connector
python -m pytest -q tests/
```

### Test layout

- `tests/test_connector.py` — the primary suite. One `test_*` function per public connector method, covering the happy path, the empty-result path, and the error path for each.
- `tests/__init__.py` — package marker; no shared fixtures live here.
- HTTP responses are mocked with `httpx.MockTransport` or `pytest-httpx` so the tests are deterministic and fast.

### Test coverage at a glance

This connector ships **148** test functions. The suite covers authentication, pagination, normalization, the connector's main API methods, and the error paths for each one.

### Integration tests

Integration tests are gated by the presence of credentials in the environment (e.g. `RUN_INTEGRATION=1` plus the connector's install-field values prefixed with `DROPBOX_`). When the gate is absent, the suite short-circuits with a `pytest.skip` so CI doesn't fail in environments without live access.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### `AuthStatus.MISSING_CREDENTIALS` — app key or secret missing

**Cause:** `client_id` (or `app_key`) and/or `client_secret` (or `app_secret`) are absent from config.

**Fix:** Both fields are required at install time. The access token is optional at install (the user can authorize later), but `client_id` and `client_secret` must always be present.

### `AuthStatus.INVALID_CREDENTIALS` — 401 token rejected

**Cause:** The `access_token` is expired, revoked, or was generated for a different app.

**Fix:**
1. Call `connector.authorize()` to generate a new authorization URL.
2. Complete the OAuth flow to obtain a fresh `access_token`.
3. Update the config with the new token.

### No access token — install returns CONNECTED but sync fails

**Cause:** Only `client_id` and `client_secret` were provided. No `access_token` is present yet.

**Fix:** Call `connector.authorize()` to get the authorization URL, direct the user to it, then exchange the returned `code` for an access token via `POST https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token`. Store the resulting `access_token` in config.

### `DropboxNotFoundError` — path error on list_folder

**Cause:** The specified path does not exist in the user's Dropbox. Dropbox returns 409 for path-not-found errors (not 404).

**Fix:** Verify the path exists in the Dropbox account. For the root, use an empty string `""` (not `"/"`).

### `ConnectorHealth.OFFLINE` after repeated network errors

**Cause:** The circuit breaker opened after 5 consecutive failures (`CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD = 5`).

**Fix:** Verify network connectivity to `api.dropboxapi.com` and `content.dropboxapi.com`. Once the underlying network issue is resolved, a successful `health_check()` call will reset the circuit breaker via `on_success()`.

### Sync finds files but no content is indexed

**Cause:** The connector syncs file **metadata** only (name, path, size, modified date). It does not download file content. The knowledge base receives `ConnectorDocument` objects with metadata fields.

**Fix:** This is by design — the Dropbox connector indexes file metadata for search and discovery. To index file content, the Shielva runtime's ingestion layer handles content extraction from the normalized documents.

sections.title: FAQ
sections.content:
### Does this connector support multiple accounts of the same provider?

Yes. Each Dropbox account is a separate connector *instance* with its own `connector_id`, install fields, and encrypted credentials. The provider key in the catalog is shared, but the multi-tenant data layer keeps the instances strictly isolated.

### Can I sync only some resource types?

Yes. The connector exposes a `sync_resources` config option (a list of resource type strings). When set, `sync()` walks only the listed types and skips the rest. Leave it unset to sync everything the connector knows about.

### How does deletion propagate?

On each `sync()`, the connector compares the set of `source_id`s returned by Dropbox against the set it last persisted to the knowledge base. IDs that are absent on the new walk are deleted from the knowledge base, so deletions in Dropbox propagate to Shielva on the next sync.

### What happens during a rate-limit hit?

The connector inspects `Retry-After` and waits the indicated interval. If the header is absent, it falls back to exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s) with full jitter, retrying up to 5 times. After that it raises a `RateLimitError` so the caller can decide whether to back off further.

### Are credentials encrypted at rest?

Yes — AES-256-GCM with a per-tenant data encryption key derived via HKDF-SHA256 from the master key. Plaintext exists in memory only for the lifetime of an in-flight request; the secret never appears in logs, audit entries, or telemetry.

### How do I add a new method?

1. Add the method to the `DropboxConnector` class in `connector.py`.
2. Add a corresponding `test_<method>` to `tests/test_connector.py` covering happy/empty/error paths.
3. Re-run the documentation generator — the new method will appear in **API Methods** automatically.

### Can the connector run in a worker / serverless context?

Yes. The connector is fully `async` and holds no global state, so it is safe to instantiate per-request (or per-task) inside Celery, AWS Lambda, or a worker pool. For long-running processes, instantiate once and reuse — the underlying HTTP client maintains a keep-alive connection pool.

sections.title: Changelog
sections.content:
All notable changes to the **Dropbox connector** are recorded here. This project follows [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/): `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` where `MAJOR` is incremented for breaking interface changes, `MINOR` for backwards-compatible features, and `PATCH` for bug fixes.

## [1.0.0] — Current

### Added
- Initial release of the Dropbox connector.
- Full sync of all primary resource types with normalization into `ConnectorDocument`.
- Public connector methods for direct API access (see **API Methods**).
- Typed exception hierarchy with retry-aware classification (see **Error Handling**).
- Health check endpoint for fast credential validation.

### Stability
- Multi-tenant safe — every query is scoped by `tenant_id`.
- Idempotent ingestion — deterministic `source_id` ensures resyncs don't duplicate.
- Circuit breaker protects upstream during sustained outage.

### Upgrade notes
- This is the initial release; no upgrade path is required.
- Future PATCH releases are guaranteed not to break existing config or method signatures.
- MINOR releases may introduce new optional methods or features; existing call sites continue to work unchanged.
- MAJOR releases will be accompanied by a migration guide in this section.
